lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Art Critic Jacques
lines.text: Ah, many greetings, and welcome to the Museum!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Art Critic Jacques
lines.text: My name is Jacques and I am the Museum's finest art critic!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Looks to me like you're the only one.

lines.speaker: Art Critic Jacques
lines.text: This is true. However, in the future, I will have my own entire floor of the Museum with paintings galore!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... has that been approved, or are you just dreaming?

lines.speaker: Art Critic Jacques
lines.text: Ah, I see you're being witty, eh?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am?

lines.speaker: Art Critic Jacques
lines.text: Yes! You're poking fun at me! I shall have my gallery. You will see eventually. Then I shall be the one with the last laugh!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you do here?

lines.speaker: Art Critic Jacques
lines.text: I critique the art! See the beautiful colours and the masterful use of the brush to capture perfectly the royal presence...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... it's a painting.

lines.speaker: Art Critic Jacques
lines.text: Just a painting? How could you say such a thing? When you learn how to appreciate the wonderful works of art, you may talk to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I see!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And look at the use of shade and contrast - isn't it just magnificent?

lines.speaker: Art Critic Jacques
lines.text: So it is. So perfect, it almost makes me weep with joy! The grandiloquent strokes of the brush convey a kind of mystic quality... It is almost as if he were here with us.

lines.speaker: Art Critic Jacques
lines.text: No words can truly describe my feelings, so let us continue to admire this work in silence.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Art Critic Jacques
lines.text: Ahh many greetinks and welcomes to ze Museum!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Art Critic Jacques
lines.text: Ahhh, mon amie! My name iz Jacques and I am ze Museum's finest art critic!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Looks to me like you're the ONLY one.

lines.speaker: Art Critic Jacques
lines.text: Zis iz true, mon amie. 'owever, in ze future, I will 'ave my own entire floor of ze Museum with paintings galore!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uhh... Has that been approved or are you just dreaming?

lines.speaker: Art Critic Jacques
lines.text: Ah, I see [madame/monsieur] iz 'aving ze little bon mot, eh?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err...bone what?

lines.speaker: Art Critic Jacques
lines.text: Ze bon mot, ze laugh, ze fun of poking at me, eh? I shall 'ave my gallery, you vill see eventually, mon amie. Zen I shall be ze, 'ow you say, laughing last!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you do here?

lines.speaker: Art Critic Jacques
lines.text: I critique ze art! See the beautifool colours and ze masterful use of ze brush to capture perfectly ze royal presence...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Umm...it's just a painting.

lines.speaker: Art Critic Jacques
lines.text: JUST a painting? 'ow could you say such a t'ing? When you learn 'ow to appreciate ze wonderful works of art, you may talk to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I see!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And look at the use of shade and contrast - isn't it just magnificent?

lines.speaker: Art Critic Jacques
lines.text: Oui...so eet eez. So perfect it almost makes me weep wiz joy! The grandiloquent strokes of ze brush convey a kind of mystic quality...eet iz almost as if 'e were 'ere with us.

lines.speaker: Art Critic Jacques
lines.text: My father, 'e too waz ze critic of art...'e also saw ze poetry in ze painting. Zis reminds me of a verse 'e would read me - would you like to 'ear it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Art Critic Jacques
lines.text: Ahh, good. Zis verse is from a poem by a most talented writer of ze poetry, Charles Baudelaire. My father would place me on 'is knee and read to me like zis...

lines.speaker: Art Critic Jacques
lines.text: Je trône dans l'azur comme un sphinx incompris; J'unis un coeur de neige à la blancheur des cygnes; Je hais le mouvement qui déplace les lignes, Et jamais je ne pleure et jamais je ne ris.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That was nice, what did it mean?

lines.speaker: Art Critic Jacques
lines.text: Ahh yes, I forget, open ze ears and I shall tell [madame/monsieur].

lines.speaker: Art Critic Jacques
lines.text: Swan-white of heart, a sphinx no mortal knows, My throne is in the heaven's azure deep, I hate all movements that disturb my pose, I smile not ever, neither do I weep.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's powerful stuff...and yes, I see what you mean about the painting inspiring you to remember that poem...

lines.speaker: Art Critic Jacques
lines.text: I am glad you enjoyed. Please, take ze time to appreciate ze Museum and ze wealth of information 'ere. Come visit me when I 'ave my own gallery!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I still didn't like it.

lines.speaker: Art Critic Jacques
lines.text: Eet iz such a shame, but you must 'ave your own opinion, no? Feel free to look around ze Museum some more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Beautiful.

lines.speaker: Art Critic Jacques
lines.text: Ahh yes, it eez. Feel free to look around ze Museum some more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I didn't like it, but I still like the painting.

lines.speaker: Art Critic Jacques
lines.text: Ahh well, eet iz not for everyone but I am glad you like ze painting.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Art Critic Jacques
lines.text: Ahh well, eet iz not for everyone.
